criteria.criterion: Criterion A: ≥5 of 9 symptoms during the same 2-week period, representing a change from prior functioning. At least one symptom must be (1) depressed mood or (2) loss of interest or pleasure (anhedonia). Symptoms (each present nearly every day unless otherwise noted): 1. Depressed mood most of the day (subjective report or observed; in children/adolescents may present as irritable mood). 2. Markedly diminished interest or pleasure in all or almost all activities most of the day. 3. Significant unintentional weight change (e.g., >5% of body weight in a month) or appetite change. 4. Insomnia or hypersomnia. 5. Psychomotor agitation or retardation (observable by others, not merely subjective). 6. Fatigue or loss of energy. 7. Feelings of worthlessness or excessive/inappropriate guilt (may be delusional; not merely self-reproach about being ill). 8. Diminished ability to think or concentrate, or indecisiveness. 9. Recurrent thoughts of death, recurrent suicidal ideation without a specific plan, or a suicide attempt or specific plan.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty nostalgic and down recently... These feelings have been persistent... confirms that the down feelings have lasted most of the day, nearly every day, for at least the past two weeks... confirms they have noticed changes in their sleep, energy, appetite, or ability to concentrate during the same two-week period... explicitly requests that the clinician follow up on this detail.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B: Functional impairment: the symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.
criteria.evidence: Patient confirms they have been feeling more restless, keyed up, or on edge than usual during the past two weeks, describing it as a pretty difficult time.

criteria.criterion: Criterion C: Substance/medical exclusion: the episode is not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.
criteria.evidence: No patient report on substance use or medical conditions provided.

criteria.criterion: Criterion D: The episode is not better explained by schizoaffective disorder, schizophrenia, schizophreniform disorder, delusional disorder, or other specified/unspecified schizophrenia spectrum and psychotic disorders.
criteria.evidence: No patient report on psychotic symptoms or related disorders provided.

criteria.criterion: Criterion E: There has never been a manic or hypomanic episode (unless these were substance-induced or attributable to a medical condition).
criteria.evidence: No patient report on history of mania or hypomania provided.

criteria.criterion: Criterion A: Excessive anxiety and worry (apprehensive expectation), occurring more days than not for at least 6 months, about a number of events or activities (e.g., work or school performance).
criteria.evidence: Patient confirms they have been feeling more restless, keyed up, or on edge than usual during the past two weeks, describing it as a pretty difficult time. No mention of 6-month duration or multiple events/activities.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B: The individual finds it difficult to control the worry.
criteria.evidence: No patient report on difficulty controlling worry provided.

criteria.criterion: Criterion C: The anxiety and worry are associated with ≥3 of the following 6 symptoms (with at least some symptoms present more days than not for the past 6 months). Only 1 item is required in children: 1. Restlessness or feeling keyed up or on edge. 2. Being easily fatigued. 3. Difficulty concentrating or mind going blank. 4. Irritability. 5. Muscle tension. 6. Sleep disturbance (difficulty falling/staying asleep, or restless, unsatisfying sleep).
criteria.evidence: Patient confirms they have been feeling more restless, keyed up, or on edge than usual during the past two weeks... confirms they have noticed changes in their sleep, energy, appetite, or ability to concentrate during the same two-week period. Only restlessness reported so far; 6-month duration not established.

criteria.criterion: Criterion D: The anxiety, worry, or physical symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.
criteria.evidence: Patient describes it as a pretty difficult time.

criteria.criterion: Criterion E: Not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance (drug of abuse, medication) or another medical condition (e.g., hyperthyroidism).
criteria.evidence: No patient report on substance use or medical conditions provided.

criteria.criterion: Criterion F: Not better explained by another mental disorder—e.g., worry about panic attacks (panic disorder), negative evaluation (social anxiety disorder), contamination/obsessions (OCD), separation from attachment figures (separation anxiety disorder), trauma reminders (PTSD), weight gain (anorexia nervosa), physical complaints (somatic symptom disorder), perceived appearance flaws (body dysmorphic disorder), having a serious illness (illness anxiety disorder), or delusional content (schizophrenia/delusional disorder).
criteria.evidence: No patient report on other mental disorders provided.
